definition: Fiscal consolidation >2pp GDP elevates recession probability to roughly double the unconditional base rate (~55-65% vs. ~27%), but with N=7 episodes, the 95% Wilson CI is [25%, 84%] — we cannot reject the null that consolidation has no effect. The 2013 sequester (~1.5-2pp drag, no recession) is a significant counterexample.
evidence: 7 episodes ≥2pp consolidation since 1947: 4-5 followed by recession (57-71%)
Unconditional recession base rate: ~27% in any 8-quarter window
2013 sequester: widely predicted recession did not materialize (GDP +1.8%, S&P +30%)
Canada 1994-97: 4pp consolidation followed by expansion (currency depreciation + U.S. demand)
1990s and 2010s non-recessions coincided with monetary easing offset
open_questions: Is the 4-6 quarter lag structure real or false precision?
Does the 2013 sequester prove the threshold is wrong, or that monetary offset saved it?
What is the speed-severity tradeoff (fast >2pp/yr: 4/5 recessions; slow <1pp/yr: 1/4)?
